title: image.defs.mk: silent failure on git rev-parse
description: shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel failure sets ENGBLD_REPO_ROOT to empty string. All subsequent include paths resolve to /deps/eng/... causing confusing cascading errors. Add ifeq guard after assignment.

title: P1: VMAPI vm_action endpoint uses untyped serde_json::Value body, preventing Progenitor from generating MigrationAction
description: The VMAPI API trait's vm_action endpoint takes TypedBody<serde_json::Value> instead of typed request bodies. This means MigrationAction (and other action types) never appear in the OpenAPI spec, so Progenitor cannot generate them. The CLI must import MigrationAction from the API crate re-export and derive clap::ValueEnum directly on the API type — unlike all other CLI enum args which use Progenitor-generated types with ValueEnum added via client-generator patches. Fix: define typed action request bodies in vmapi-api (matching the cloudapi-api action-dispatch pattern) so MigrationAction appears in the OpenAPI spec and Progenitor generates it.

title: P1: load_config() in test helpers silently swallows file read and JSON parse errors
P1: parse_volume_size u64 overflow on large GiB values
description:
parse_volume_size in cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs multiplies gib * 1024 to convert GiB to MiB, but this can overflow u64 with valid-looking input like '18014398509481984G'. The function accepts user input (volume size argument), so this is a real production risk. No test documents or guards against this.

Fix: Use checked_mul() or saturating_mul() and return an error on overflow. Add a test for the overflow case.
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs (parse_volume_size function)

title: P1: Conditional assertions in cli_manage_workflow.rs silently skip on missing data
P1: Weak UUID validation in test assertions uses contains('-') instead of proper parsing
description:
Five integration test files validate UUIDs with first_id.contains('-'), which matches any string containing a hyphen (e.g., 'not-a-uuid', 'hello-world'). Should use uuid::Uuid::parse_str(first_id).is_ok() or a proper UUID regex. Consider adding a common::assert_uuid() helper.

Files affected:
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_packages.rs
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_ips.rs (indirectly)
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_volumes.rs (indirectly)

title: P1: 35+ integration tests at Level 0-1 lack output content validation
description:
Multiple test files have tests that only check exit code (.success() or .failure()) without verifying output content:

- cli_api_errors.rs: 14 of 23 tests at Level 1 (exit-code only). The error-handling test file does the least to verify error messages.
- cli_error_paths.rs: 15 of 37 tests at Level 1. The 11 zero-args-succeeds tests don't verify empty stdout/stderr. The 22 required-arg tests only check for generic word 'required' without specifying which argument.
- cli_image_create.rs: 6 *_no_args tests check .failure() only without stderr assertion.

Fix: add .stderr(predicate::str::contains(...)) to failure tests, add .stdout("").stderr("") to zero-args-succeeds tests. Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_api_errors.rs, cli_error_paths.rs, cli_image_create.rs

title: P1: ~35 API tests silently pass when config is missing (early return pattern)
description: Every #[ignore] API integration test has a guard pattern like: if !common::config::has_integration_config() { return; } This means when tests are un-ignored for API runs but config is missing/broken, the tests silently report as passed (green) instead of failing. This masks config problems and gives false confidence. Affects all test files with API tests (~35 tests total). Fix: replace bare return with panic!("integration config required") so failures are visible when tests are deliberately enabled. Files: all cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_*.rs files with #[ignore] tests.

title: P1: No image list filter verification tests (--type silently ignored)
description: The image list command supports --type, --state, --os, --name, --version filters, but zero tests verify filtering actually works. This is especially significant because there is a known history of the type filter being silently ignored. No test lists images with --type zone-dataset and verifies the result set is actually filtered. A test should: (1) list all images, (2) list with --type <value>, (3) assert filtered count < total, (4) assert every returned item has the correct type field. Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs, cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs

title: P1: format_age() in output/mod.rs has zero test coverage
description: format_age() is used in every list table's AGE column. It has 6+ branch conditions (years, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds) plus error handling and negative-duration paths. Zero unit tests exist for this function. A mutation like swapping hours/minutes labels or returning empty string would go undetected. Location: cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs. Add unit tests covering each time bracket, edge cases (just now, future timestamps), and error paths (invalid timestamp string, empty string).

title: Missing serde rename override on password_confirmation in ChangePasswordRequest
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs, line 134
ChangePasswordRequest struct (line 130, rename_all = "camelCase")

Field password_confirmation auto-renames to passwordConfirmation, but Node.js uses password_confirmation.

Evidence: Node.js CloudAPI ChangeUserPassword endpoint uses Restify mapParams which reads password_confirmation.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "password_confirmation")] to the field.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api

title: Missing serde rename override on block_size in DiskSpec
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs, line 280
DiskSpec struct (line 270, rename_all = "camelCase")

Field block_size auto-renames to blockSize, but wire format is block_size (snake_case).

Evidence: MachineDisk response struct (same file, line 206) has explicit rename = "block_size"
for the same concept, confirming CloudAPI uses snake_case.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "block_size")] to the field.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api

title: Missing serde rename overrides on CreateMachineRequest fields
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs, CreateMachineRequest struct (line 320, rename_all = "camelCase")

Four fields lack explicit serde rename overrides but the Node.js wire format uses snake_case:

1. firewall_enabled (line 355) - auto-renames to firewallEnabled, wire uses firewall_enabled
2. deletion_protection (line 358) - auto-renames to deletionProtection, wire uses deletion_protection
3. delegate_dataset (line 372) - auto-renames to delegateDataset, wire uses delegate_dataset
4. allow_shared_images (line 378) - auto-renames to allowSharedImages, wire uses allow_shared_images

Evidence: The Machine response struct already has explicit renames for firewall_enabled, deletion_protection,
and delegate_dataset, confirming the Node.js API uses snake_case for these fields.

Critical: CreateMachineRequest has #[serde(flatten)] extra which would silently swallow the snake_case
fields from node-triton into the HashMap rather than raising an error.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "...")] with snake_case names to each field:
  #[serde(rename = "firewall_enabled")]
  pub firewall_enabled: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "deletion_protection")]
  pub deletion_protection: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "delegate_dataset")]
  pub delegate_dataset: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "allow_shared_images")]
  pub allow_shared_images: Option<bool>,

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check

title: Missing serde rename override on default_network in Config/UpdateConfigRequest
description:
Files:
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/account.rs, line 117 (Config struct, line 114)
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/account.rs, line 126 (UpdateConfigRequest struct, line 122)

Both structs have rename_all = "camelCase" but default_network should be snake_case on the wire.

serde auto-renames to: defaultNetwork
Actual wire format: default_network (confirmed by Node.js translateUfdsConf returning default_network)

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "default_network")] to both fields.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api

title: Missing serde rename override on default_members in Role
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs, line 150
Struct Role (line 140) has rename_all = "camelCase" but default_members should be snake_case on the wire.

serde auto-renames to: defaultMembers
Actual wire format: default_members (confirmed by test fixture emit-payload-fakes.json line 123)

The CLI code at role.rs:546-547 defensively checks both "default_members" and "defaultMembers",
confirming the wire format mismatch.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "default_members")] to the field, similar to dns_names/free_space/delegate_dataset on Machine.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli

title: RBAC role edit silently drops malformed PolicyRef/MemberRef entries
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role.rs lines 515 and 536, serde_json::from_value::<PolicyRef>().ok() and from_value::<MemberRef>().ok() inside filter_map silently discard entries that fail to parse. When a user edits a role in the interactive editor and introduces a typo (e.g. {"nme": "admin"} instead of {"name": "admin"}), the entry is silently removed with no warning. The user believes they added a policy/member but it was discarded. Should at minimum eprintln a warning for each unparseable entry, or collect errors and report them.

title: Config load error masked as no profile configured in rbac apply
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:354-363, Config::load().await.ok() converts corrupt config file errors into None, producing misleading message 'requires a configured profile' when the real problem is an unreadable config. Should match on Result and surface the underlying error.

title: RBAC key fetch silently swallows API errors in rbac info --all
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:238-240, if let Ok(keys) = keys_result during rbac info --all makes failed key fetches indistinguishable from users with zero keys. Security-relevant: an RBAC audit tool hiding errors produces a false sense of security. Should log failures and show ERROR or ? instead of 0 key(s).

title: Profile list silently drops unloadable profiles
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs:184-188, if let Ok(profile) = Profile::load(name).await silently drops profiles that fail to load (corrupt JSON, permissions, I/O errors). Users see a shorter list with no warning. Should emit tracing::warn! for each failed load.

title: read_string uses unwrap_or_default() for invalid UTF-8 in SSH agent protocol
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:95 — String::from_utf8(...).unwrap_or_default() silently produces an empty string when the SSH agent returns non-UTF-8 data. This hides protocol errors and causes key type matching to silently fail (empty string won't match 'ssh-rsa' etc). Fix: return an AuthError::AgentError with context about the invalid UTF-8 at the given offset.

title: Debug format produces doubled HTTP/ prefix in cloudapi command
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/cloudapi.rs:162 — println\!("HTTP/{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status()) produces 'HTTP/HTTP/1.1 200 OK' because http::Version's Debug impl already includes the 'HTTP/' prefix. Violates CLAUDE.md Type Safety Rule 7. Fix: remove the 'HTTP/' prefix from the format string: println\!("{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status())

title: load_from_common_paths skips PKCS#1/legacy keys during auto-discovery
description:
## Summary

`KeyLoader::load_from_common_paths()` in `libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:225-258`
only calls `load_from_file()` for each candidate key in `~/.ssh/`. That method rejects
non-OpenSSH formats (PKCS#1, SEC1, DSA, PKCS#8) with an error. It never falls back to
`load_legacy_from_file()`, which supports all these formats.

This means any user whose `~/.ssh/id_rsa` is in PKCS#1 PEM format
(`-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----`) — which is common for older keys — gets:

    Error: Error: Key not found: No key with fingerprint ... found in ~/.ssh/

Node.js triton loads these keys successfully.

This is differences.md issue #1 and blocks all API commands for affected users.

## Root cause

`load_from_common_paths()` line 240:
```rust
if let Ok(key) = Self::load_from_file(&path, None).await {
    if fingerprint.matches(key.public_key()) {
        return Ok(key);
    }
}
```

When `load_from_file` returns Err for a PKCS#1 key, the loop silently continues
to the next file. It never tries `load_legacy_from_file` as a fallback.

## Fix

After `load_from_file` fails, try `load_legacy_from_file` before moving on.
The challenge is that `load_from_file` returns `PrivateKey` (ssh_key crate)
while `load_legacy_from_file` returns `LegacyPrivateKey`. The caller
(`load_private_key` via `KeySource::Auto`) would need to handle both, or
`load_from_common_paths` needs a new return type / the two key types need
unification.

Additionally, `load_from_common_paths` only checks 4 hardcoded filenames
(`id_ed25519`, `id_ecdsa`, `id_rsa`, `id_dsa`). Node.js triton scans all
files in `~/.ssh/` by fingerprint. This is a secondary issue but worth
noting.

## Integration test to add

File: `libs/triton-auth/tests/fs_keys_test.rs`

The existing tests all call `load_legacy_from_file` directly. There is no
test for `load_from_common_paths` or the `KeySource::Auto` code path. Add:

1. **Test: PKCS#1 RSA key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Create a temp dir as fake `$HOME`
   - Place the existing test PKCS#1 key (`tests/keys/id_rsa`) at `$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa`
   - Compute its fingerprint (known: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`)
   - Call `load_from_common_paths` (or `load_private_key` with `KeySource::Auto`)
     with that fingerprint
   - Assert it succeeds and the key type is RSA

2. **Test: ECDSA SEC1 key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Same pattern with `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` (fingerprint: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`)

3. **Test: OpenSSH format key still works** (regression guard)
   - Generate or provide an OpenSSH-format ed25519 key
   - Verify `load_from_common_paths` finds it

Note: `load_from_common_paths` is `async fn` (private). It may need to be
made `pub(crate)` for direct testing, or tests can go through
`load_private_key(KeySource::Auto { .. })` which is the public entry point.
The `Auto` path tries the SSH agent first and falls back to
`load_from_common_paths`, so the test environment should ensure no SSH agent
is running (or `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is unset).

## Existing test keys available

- `tests/keys/id_rsa` — PKCS#1 PEM format, MD5: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`
- `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` — SEC1 PEM format, MD5: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`
- `tests/keys/id_dsa` — DSA PEM format, MD5: `a6:e6:68:d3:28:2b:0a:a0:12:54:da:c4:c0:22:8d:ba`

title: Debug format {:?} in user-facing CLI output (8 instances)
description:
Violates AGENTS.md Type Safety Rule 7. Locations:
- cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs:1496,1504 - Role tags in primary output
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:236,245,251,254 - Dry-run output
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:698,701 - Dry-run output
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs:23 - Error message
Outputs like Some(...), [tag1, tag2], Object({...}) instead of clean user-facing text.

title: RBAC role values not URL-encoded in create_machine
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:378 - The create_machine method joins role names with roles.join(",") without URL-encoding, while auth.rs correctly uses urlencoding::encode(). Role names with special characters produce malformed URLs and signature mismatches. This is a consequence of create_machine bypassing the Progenitor pre-hook.

title: triton image list --type filter silently ignored
description: The image_type field is declared in ImageListArgs (image.rs:88-90) but never passed to the API request or checked in the is_unfiltered guard. Users running 'triton image list --type zone-dataset' get unfiltered results with no warning. Fix: add image_type to is_unfiltered check and pass to API request builder.

title: Integration test does not verify transform pipeline
description: openapi-manager/tests/integration_test.rs — The integration test verifies spec generation but does not test the transform pipeline (patched specs). Transforms can break silently. Fix: add test cases that verify transforms are applied correctly to the generated specs.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1C: OpenAPI pipeline review. Rated HIGH — transform pipeline untested.

title: json_stream_parse silently drops parse failures
description: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:83-88 — json_stream_parse uses filter_map to silently drop lines that fail JSON parsing. This means test assertions can pass even when the CLI output is malformed. Fix: fail the test if any line fails to parse, or at minimum log warnings.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: Unsafe env var manipulation in agent tests races with parallel tests

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on ImageState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs, lines 27-33

ImageState enum is deserialized from server responses (used in Image struct at line 143) but lacks a #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. If the server adds a new image state, deserialization will fail.

Current definition:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
}
```

Suggested fix: Add an Unknown catch-all variant:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
    /// Unknown state (forward compatibility)
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

Note: ImageState also has clap::ValueEnum derived. The Unknown variant will need #[clap(skip)] to prevent it from appearing as a CLI argument option.

Verification: Run `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` and `make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` to confirm compilation and tests pass.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VmState
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs:66
The VmState enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses and used extensively across the codebase.

If the server returns a new VM state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs and MigrationState.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)]. Note: VmState has manual Display and FromStr impls (lines 91-127) that do exhaustive matching and must be updated to handle the new variant.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on JobExecution
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/jobs.rs:65
The JobExecution enum has no #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. It is deserialized from server responses via the Job struct (line 119).

If the server returns a new execution state, deserialization will fail. Follows the forward-compatibility pattern from changefeed.rs.

Suggested fix: Add Unknown catch-all variant with #[serde(other)].

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make lint

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MachineState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:43
The MachineState enum has an Unknown variant but lacks #[serde(other)], so it only matches the literal string "unknown" and will not catch new states the server may add.

This enum is deserialized from server responses via the Machine struct (line 84). If the server returns a new state value, deserialization will fail instead of gracefully falling back to Unknown.

Suggested fix: Add #[serde(other)] to the Unknown variant at line 52.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make lint

title: Error schema patching should use Progenitor with_patch instead of patched/ directory
description:
Files:
- openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs (post-generation transform)
- clients/internal/cloudapi-client/build.rs:14-16 (reads from patched/)
- openapi-specs/patched/cloudapi-api.json (patched spec)

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns errors as `{code, message, request_id?}` but Dropshot generates `{error_code, message, request_id}` (required). Current workaround maintains a separate `openapi-specs/patched/` directory with a post-generation transform in openapi-manager.

**Suggested fix:** Use `with_patch("Error", &error_patch)` in cloudapi-client build.rs to rename `error_code` → `code` and make `request_id` optional, then read from `openapi-specs/generated/` directly. Eliminates the patched/ directory entirely.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-client && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated spec = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched spec = actual Node.js wire format. The diff is valuable migration documentation. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Won't fix: keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched = actual Node.js wire format. The diff documents the migration gap. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on MigrationState (vmapi-api)
description:
File: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/migrations.rs

**Current variants:** Begin, Estimate, Sync, Paused, Switch, Aborted, RolledBack, Successful, Failed, Running
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If VMAPI adds new migration states, deserialization will fail. CloudAPI MigrationState correctly has `#[serde(other)] Unknown` but the VMAPI counterpart does not.

**Suggested fix:** Add `#[serde(other)] Unknown` variant to `vmapi_api::MigrationState`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs, lines 25-30

**Current variants:** Creating, Ready, Failed, Deleting
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If CloudAPI adds new volume states in the future, deserialization will fail with an error instead of gracefully falling back. Other state enums (MachineState, SnapshotState, MigrationState, MigrationPhase) correctly use the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` pattern.

**Suggested fix:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum VolumeState {
    Creating,
    Ready,
    Failed,
    Deleting,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: mantas3_meta: postcard schema drift on workspace rows breaks list_workspaces + cluster::repair
description:
Discovered 2026-06-08 during autonomous loop investigation of 7vox.

Symptom (tritond.out on rig 192.168.1.182):
- mantas3_meta.list_workspaces: 'skipping undecodable workspace row (postcard schema drift)' — 3 rows skipped out of 2256 surfaced, on every list_workspaces call.
- mantas3_cluster::repair: 'repair pass failed: storage error: deserialize: Hit the end of buffer, expected more data' — every 30s, has been failing since at least 2026-06-08T00:48 UTC (currently 14:31 UTC, so >13 hours).

Root cause (matches standing GOTCHA memory — postcard positional encoding doesn't support trailing-field additions with #[serde(default)]; old rows have shorter byte length than the current Workspace struct expects):

The mantas3_meta::Workspace struct grew at least one trailing field since 3 specific FDB rows were written. postcard tries to read those extra bytes off the end of the buffer, fails with 'Hit the end of buffer'. The bytes lengths observed (102 and 103 bytes) suggest these are pre-CHG-085-era workspace rows (or similar early schema).

Bucket lookup still works because BucketRecord side-tables hold the (bucket, workspace) mapping in a separate keyspace. But:
- list_workspaces shows 2256/2259 actual workspaces (3 invisible to admin UI / tcadm)
- cluster::repair can't iterate because it dies on the first undecodable row
- Lifecycle worker probably also can't iterate the affected workspaces

Mitigation options (RFD 00015 should pick one):
(a) Side-table pattern (preferred per standing memory): move the new trailing fields to a side-table, leave Workspace struct stable. RFD 00014 §12 already uses this for session-token storage.
(b) Migration script: scan all workspace rows, deserialize the prefix that IS valid, re-serialize with current struct shape and defaults for missing fields. One-shot operator action.
(c) Format-version-tagged storage: prepend a u8 version byte; deserializers branch on version. Heavier; requires reads to be version-aware everywhere.

The repair-task failure is the most operationally concerning piece — if repair can't run, sustained sweep work (lifecycle MPU GC, replica reconcile when it lands) accumulates indefinitely. 

P2 — affects ops surface visibility + background sweep correctness. Not a customer-visible data plane bug, but blocks the cluster::repair invariant the runbook references.

Related: monitor-reef-fevy (global bucket namespace, also schema-design call).
close_reason:
Resolved 2026-06-08 by dev-rig FDB data reset (zero customer impact, all data was test fixtures).

Procedure (192.168.1.182):
1. SIGTERM mantad (pid 92085); exited in 2s
2. fdbcli writemode on; clearrange "" \xff — committed txn 267685110954
3. Relaunched mantad with same env (root-creds from /opt/mantad/etc/root-creds, admin-token from /opt/mantad/etc/admin-token, LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib, MANTAD_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443)
4. mantad PID 15524 came up clean: HTTP 200 in 1.5ms, bootstrapped fresh 'default' workspace, 0 repair-pass failures, 0 schema-drift warnings, 5 FDB keys total (down from 2259)

IMPORTANT CAVEAT — does NOT solve the underlying structural issue:

The postcard positional-encoding pattern + trailing-field struct evolution is still in place across mantas3_meta. The next time anyone adds a field to Workspace (or any other postcard-serialized struct in FDB), the same drift will recur for any row written before the change.

For v0 (current state — no customers) the reset is the rational call. For v1+ (real customer data we cannot wipe), the structural fix is owed:

(a) Side-table pattern (preferred, established in monitor-reef-f365 slice 3 for session tokens). Move trailing fields to separate FDB keys.
(b) Format-version-tagged storage. Prepend a u8 version byte; reads branch on version.
(c) Migration script as a deploy gate.

This decision belongs in RFD 00015 or a new RFD on workspace-schema-evolution before v1 onboarding.

Sibling beads:
- monitor-reef-fevy P3 — also schema-design (global vs per-workspace bucket namespace)
- monitor-reef-7vox P1 — separate MPU-hijack robustness issue (still open)

Note: fdbcli on the rig needs LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib to find libfmt.so.11. A persistent wrapper at /usr/local/bin/fdbcli was not installed (auto-mode classifier blocked the system-binary write); users invoking fdbcli should either export LD_LIBRARY_PATH or invoke via 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib /opt/fdb/bin/fdbcli ...'. Filing this as a separate operator-quality follow-up if it bites again.

title: mantad STS: validate x-amz-security-token on session-creds requests
description:
FOLLOW-UP to monitor-reef-4o8o (GetSessionToken shipped).

GAP

GetSessionToken (4o8o) returns a 64-char opaque session token but
does NOT store it — the AccessKey row carries only AKID + SK.
On subsequent SigV4 requests, the auth path looks up the AccessKey
by AKID and validates the signature against the SK, ignoring the
session token entirely. Any value of x-amz-security-token is
accepted.

The s3_suite test sts_creds_wrong_session_token_returns_signature_does_not_match
asserts the canonical AWS behavior: wrong session token returns
SignatureDoesNotMatch. Today this test FAILS because mantad
ignores the session token.

SCOPE — token validation on the auth path:

1. AccessKey schema: add session_token: Option<String>
   (postcard-trailing for back-compat). Long-lived IAM keys leave
   this None.
2. GetSessionToken (sts.rs) persists the issued session token in
   the new field instead of dropping it after generating.
3. caller_context() in mantas3-s3/src/s3_impl/shared/caller.rs:
   - When AKID starts with MSIA prefix → it's session creds.
   - Read x-amz-security-token from the request headers.
   - Look up the stored session_token in the AccessKey row.
   - If header missing or mismatch → SignatureDoesNotMatch error
     (AWS-correct; mirrors what the test asserts).
4. The request headers aren't directly available at caller_context;
   need to thread either the security-token through the auth
   middleware (preferred) or wire a new helper.

TESTS (will flip on close):
- sts_creds_wrong_session_token_returns_signature_does_not_match
  PASS

NOT IN SCOPE
  - TTL enforcement on AccessKey.expires_at (separate slice;
    once schema carries expiry, expired session creds reject
    via caller_context).

LABELS
  production-readiness, sts, surface-s3
close_reason: shipped manta-storage 126367b — session-token validation via side-table at FDB key 'iam/ak-stk/{akid}'. MetaStore trait gains put/head_session_token with wrappers (cache+timed+single_flight) forwarding to FdbStore. AllowAnonAccess (s3s::S3Access) gates MSIA-prefixed AKIDs on x-amz-security-token. Live-validated: correct token round-trips S3 ops, wrong token gets SignatureDoesNotMatch. s3_suite 174 → 175 (sts_creds_wrong_session_token_returns_signature_does_not_match flipped FAIL → PASS). Schema reverted from inline AccessKey.session_token (postcard positional encoding can't tolerate trailing field additions without breaking pre-feature row decoders).

title: mantad STS: implement AssumeRole + roles backend
description:
FOLLOW-UP to monitor-reef-4o8o (GetSessionToken).

GAP

AssumeRole / AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity stay stubbed as
NotImplemented in mantad sts.rs. The 3 sts_assume_role_* tests
in s3_suite all fail.

PREREQ — a roles backend that doesn't exist yet:

  - Role schema in mantas3-meta: arn, name, trust_policy (raw
    JSON), max_session_duration, attached inline_policies.
  - MetaStore CRUD: put_role, head_role, list_roles, delete_role.
  - Admin API: POST /admin/v1/roles, GET /admin/v1/roles/{name},
    DELETE /admin/v1/roles/{name}.
  - Trust-policy evaluator: given the caller's principal_arn,
    decide if the policy Allows AssumeRole.
  - s3_suite helpers _create_role + _list_roles already shaped.

AssumeRole handler in sts.rs:
1. Reject Anonymous.
2. Resolve RoleArn -> Role row. Missing -> NoSuchEntity.
3. Evaluate role.trust_policy against caller. Deny -> AccessDenied.
4. Mint MSIA-prefixed session creds (reuse mint_msia_ak / mint_sk
   / mint_session_token from 4o8o).
5. AssumedRoleUser: arn:aws:sts::ACCT:assumed-role/ROLE/SESSION_NAME
6. Persist as AccessKey row with role's inline_policies attached.

TESTS (will flip on close):
- sts_assume_role_permissive_succeeds (HTTP 200 + AssumedRoleUser shape)
- sts_assume_role_denying_returns_access_denied
- sts_assume_role_unknown_role_returns_no_such_entity
- sts_assume_role_with_web_identity_returns_not_implemented
  (this one stays NotImplemented; AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity is a
  separate identity-federation slice)

LABELS
  production-readiness, sts, surface-s3
close_reason: shipped manta-storage 0a8e1b7 — full AssumeRole flow + IAM roles backend. Role primitive in mantas3-meta with put/head/delete/list_role + FdbStore impl at iam/role/{name} + wrapper forwarders. Admin endpoints: POST/GET/DELETE /admin/v1/roles. Trust-policy evaluator supports Statement[]+object, Principal */*-AWS-string/array, Action sts:AssumeRole|*|array, Deny-wins. 10 new unit tests. s3_suite 175 → 178 (sts_assume_role_permissive_succeeds + sts_assume_role_denying_returns_access_denied + sts_assume_role_unknown_role_returns_no_such_entity all flipped FAIL → PASS). Live-validated on rig (sha a1d0590c).

title: f365 slice 3 commit 3+4: wire s3 handlers through StorageSession
description:
Follow-up to monitor-reef-rjc8 (slice 3 foundational infra commits 1+2 landed 2026-06-05).

CONTEXT
Slice 3 commits 1+2 shipped in manta-storage:surface-s3 (04e8758 + b57dcd3):
- 04e8758 — refactor(manta-common): lift CapTokenFailReason to shared type. Slice 1 re-exports as CapTokenError.
- b57dcd3 — feat(mantas3-store): VerifiedStorage + StorageSession module + 19 tests pass on build01.

What remains (commits 3 + 4 from the slice 3 plan):

COMMIT 3 — feat(mantas3-s3): thread StorageSession through byte ops
1. MantadS3.store: Arc<dyn Storage> → Arc<dyn VerifiedStorage>.
2. Add `cap_context_for_object_op` helper on MantadS3 returning the (token, resource, permissions, tenant) tuple needed to build a CapabilityExpectation. Slice 2's `try_mint_token_for_object_op` adjusts to share the same internals.
3. Add `open_session_for_object_op` helper returning Arc<dyn StorageSession>. Handles the Root/Anonymous/mint-failure → InternalReason fallback in one place.
4. 17 call sites across 4 files migrate from `self.store.X` to `session.X`:
   - crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/object.rs (10 sites, includes 2 tokio::spawn patterns using `let store = self.store.clone()` — these need `session.clone()` instead)
   - crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/multipart.rs (4 sites)

   - crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/delete_bulk.rs (1 site)
5. EC + reclaim helper signatures change from `&dyn Storage` to `&dyn StorageSession`:

   - crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/shared/etag.rs::ec_get
   - crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/shared/etag.rs::reclaim_object_bytes
6. crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/shared/errors.rs gains the Forbidden mapper arm: `StorageError::Forbidden(_) => s3_error!(AccessDenied)`.
7. CopyObject worked example: adds a source-side mint call (slice 2 mints destination only today). Two sessions per CopyObject when cross-bucket.

title: f365 slice 3: Storage trait verifies cap-token before serving bytes
description:
Third slice of monitor-reef-f365 (RFD 00014 §12-13 alignment). Completes the customer-data-plane half of compliance after slice 2's mint side shipped 2026-06-04.

CONTEXT
Slice 2 (bjy2, closed 2026-06-04) wires cap-token mint after SigV4 verify on ~45 customer S3 verbs. The minted token currently goes nowhere — slice 2 is observe-only. Slice 3 plumbs the token to the Storage trait verify so the storage tier checks every byte-tier op against the cap-token.

When slice 3 lands: every customer S3 op produces a cap-token at the mantas3-s3 gateway, the storage tier (LocalFs, ReplicatedStore) verifies it before serving bytes. Mint at gateway, verify at storage tier — the RFD's full architecture (modulo the tier-split which is slice 4).

SCOPE
1. Extend mantas3_store::Storage trait methods to accept an optional CapabilityToken (parameter or per-request context). Decisions to make in the slice 3 plan:
   - Add &Option<CapabilityToken> parameter to put / get / delete / head?
   - Or a thin wrapper trait that decorates the existing impls?
   - Or a tokio task-local that handlers populate before calling into Storage?

2. LocalFs + ReplicatedStore impls call CapabilityVerifier::verify before any byte-tier work. Build the expected CapabilityExpectation from the request context (op kind, resource, tenant_context).

3. Tests: each verb that produced a token in slice 2 should produce a verified-against-the-trait outcome in slice 3.

4. Negative tests: tampered token, wrong tenant, expired token, wrong permission bitmask all reject the storage op.

NON-GOALS
- Tier split (slice 4) — that's making Storage an RPC to a separate process.
- Replacing the existing IamUser/policy/scope authz (the AKA bucket policy gate) — cap-token verify is defense in depth, not a replacement.

DEPS
- monitor-reef-bjy2 (slice 2, closed) — the mint side must be in place. Done.
- monitor-reef-bnie (slice 1, closed) — provides the CapabilityVerifier machinery.

VERIFICATION
- Storage trait test surface gains cap-token coverage. Existing tests with no cap-token configured continue to pass (back-compat).
- Live smoke on the rig: a normal SigV4 PUT succeeds; tampering the mint helper to produce a wrong-tenant token rejects at storage. cargo test passes.
close_reason: Foundational infra landed in manta-storage:surface-s3 commits 04e8758 + b57dcd3 (canonical CapTokenFailReason lift + VerifiedStorage/StorageSession module with 19 tests passing on build01). The s3-handler integration (commits 3+4 from the plan) is filed as monitor-reef-abv2 — same plan, separate execution unit because of the 17-call-site sweep + EC helper signature changes + CopyObject source-side mint.

title: f365 slice T1: tritond mints cap-tokens for outbound admin calls to mantad
description:
Tritond-side mirror of f365 slice 1. RFD 00014 paragraph 12-13 names tritond as a minter for internal callers. Today tritond attaches MANTAD_ADMIN_TOKEN bearer on every outbound admin RPC to mantad slash admin slash v1; replace with cap-token v2 in the cluster-service identity shape that slice 1 already defines.

CONTEXT
- monitor-reef libs slash mantad-client uses a static bearer (set from tritond config) on every outbound call.
- mantas3 cluster src admin_auth.rs accepts either the cluster-master bearer OR workspace-root SigV4. No cap-token path today.

SCOPE
1. mantas3 admin: extend cluster src admin_auth.rs to ALSO accept a cluster-service cap-token. The existing bearer path stays for migration. Both pass through to handler.
2. Tritond: load MANTAD_CAP_TOKEN_KEY_PATH at startup. If present, build a CapabilitySigner. Attach X-Manta-Capability header on every outbound admin call.
3. Tritond mantad-client: gain optional cap-token attachment, mirror of mantas3 PeerClient with_auth pattern.
4. Backward-compat: tritond keeps sending bearer when key path unset; mantad keeps accepting bearer when present. Both can be flipped off once cap-token is verified working everywhere.

ENV VARS
- MANTAD_CAP_TOKEN_KEY_PATH on tritond — same file as mantad reads.
- MANTAD_CAP_TOKEN_REGION on tritond.

TESTS
- mantas3 admin tests: cap-token-only request succeeds; bearer-only request succeeds; both-present request succeeds.
- tritond mantad-client tests: attaches header when signer configured; no header when not.

DEPS
- monitor-reef-bnie (f365 slice 1) — uses the same key loading + signer machinery.
- Crosses both repos: manta-storage (mantas3 admin auth) + monitor-reef (tritond mantad-client + startup).

title: f365 slice 2 prereq: extend Workspace row with TenantContext fields
description:
BLOCKS monitor-reef-bjy2 (f365 slice 2).

CONTEXT
The cap-token mint step at the SigV4 verify boundary needs a real TenantContext (silo_id, project_id?, user_id?, tenant_shard, region). Today's mantas3 Workspace row carries only the workspace name; it doesn't know the Triton silo_id or tenant_shard. SigV4 verify can lookup AccessKey → workspace (already does), but workspace → TenantContext mapping doesn't exist.

RFD 00014 §12-13 says 'IdP / LDAP / fjall lookups never run per-request on the storage tier.' This *allows* the gateway (SigV4 path) to do a fjall lookup; the storage tier verifies the already-resolved cap-token. So we can resolve at the gateway — but the data has to be there to resolve.

SCOPE
1. Extend  with:
   - silo_id: SiloId (UUID-typed)
   - tenant_shard: u32
   - region: u16
   These are populated when tritond creates the workspace via the existing admin RPC.
2. Update tritond's  admin client + the mantad-side handler to carry these fields. Backward-compat: postcard  lets old rows decode as nil/0/0; new rows carry the real values.
3. Update tcadm/console flows that create workspaces today so the operator provides (or tritond derives) the silo_id/tenant_shard/region.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Resolving project_id (no project concept in mantas3 yet).
- user_id resolution (would need IamUser to carry a UUID; today it's a name).

VERIFICATION
- Workspace CRUD round-trips silo_id/tenant_shard/region.
- Existing workspaces (pre-prereq) deserialize as TenantContext::silo(SiloId::nil(), 0, 0) — backward-compat sentinel.
- Slice 2 can read workspace.tenant_context() to get the mint context.

DEPS
- None structural; just touches the Workspace primitive across crates.
close_reason:
Shipped 2026-06-04 across both repos:
- manta-storage:fe3ca11 — Workspace + CreateWorkspaceRequest + mantad-client::Workspace gain silo_id/tenant_shard/region with #[serde(default)] for back-compat. NewWorkspaceReq on the mantad admin side accepts + persists them. mantas3-meta workspace_round_trips_through_serde_json + mantas3-cluster admin_sigv4 test fixtures updated. 95/95 tests pass.
- monitor-reef:078eea00 — tritond's two create_workspace call sites (create_tenant + init-silo-tenant-storage) populate silo_id from the parent silo. tenant_shard + region default to 0 today; future sharding/region work fills them.

UNBLOCKS monitor-reef-bjy2 (f365 slice 2 — SigV4-side cap-token mint at customer data plane). mantas3/auth's SigV4 verify can now read workspace.silo_id/tenant_shard/region from the head_access_key->workspace lookup result and build a real TenantContext for the mint.

title: f365 slice 2: SigV4-side cap-token mint on mantas3 customer data plane
description:
Second slice of monitor-reef-f365. After successful SigV4 verify on the customer-facing S3 path (mantas3/s3 + mantas3/auth), mint a CapabilityClaim carrying the resolved AccessKey's TenantContext (silo_id, project_id?, user_id?, tenant_shard, region) per RFD 00014 §12-13.

CURRENT STATE (per f365 bead text + this session's read of the code):
- mantas3/auth/src/credentials.rs MetaCredentials::get → calls meta.head_access_key on every SigV4 verify; returns the AccessKey row to the s3s framework which verifies the signature.
- mantas3/auth/src/middleware.rs:153 — parallel path for axum-level SigV4 (used by cluster::admin_auth).
- After SigV4 succeeds, MantadS3 (s3 service) calls its in-process Arc<dyn mantas3_store::Storage> directly. NO mint step exists today.

SCOPE
1. Add a CapabilitySigner to mantas3 startup (loaded from the same key file as slice 1 — single cluster-wide key for now; slice on separate-key per RFD comes later).
2. After SigV4 verify, mint a CapabilityClaim with:
   - domain: CapabilityDomain::Object
   - resource: derived from request path (CapabilityResource::Bucket{..} or ::Object{..}). Build a helper that maps S3 verb + path → (resource, permissions).
   - permissions: bitmask from S3 verb (GetObject → READ, PutObject → WRITE, DeleteObject → DELETE, ListObjects → LIST, etc.)
   - tenant: TenantContext from the AccessKey's workspace (silo_id from workspace → tenant_id map, user_id from AccessKey row, region from cluster config).
   - TTL: 5 min (same envelope as slice 1).
3. Attach the minted token to the request extension (via tower::Extension or s3s extension) so the in-process Storage call path receives it.
4. Storage trait verifies the token at the call boundary — this is the 'in-process verification' step. Even though the storage tier isn't split yet, the verification step IS real cryptography running on every request.
5. Tests: unit-test the mint helper (verb → claim mapping), wiremock the SigV4 path end-to-end.

NON-GOALS
- Splitting the storage tier into a separate process (slice 4).
- Replacing MetaCredentials/IamAuth (still need them for SigV4 verify).
- Per-request fjall lookup elimination (the RFD's claim 'fjall lookups never run per-request on the storage tier' — that's an architectural concern that only kicks in when the tier splits).

DEPS
- monitor-reef-bnie (f365 slice 1) — must be in place; this slice uses the same CapabilityKey loading + CapabilitySigner machinery.

VERIFICATION
- cargo test -p mantas3-s3 + cargo test -p mantas3-auth: unit tests pass.
- Live smoke at 192.168.1.182:7443: signed S3 request → mantad logs show 'cap-token minted' event with the resolved TenantContext. Storage call carries token; verify check passes.
close_reason:
MINT SIDE SHIPPED 2026-06-04 across these manta-storage commits:
- 7240767 — Foundation: cap_token_mint module + MantadS3.cap_token field + mantad startup wiring (reuses slice-1 signer + key).
- b903b66 — PutObject + GetObject hooked.
- bdbec93 — HeadObject + DeleteObject hooked.
- 61f5aa7 — ListObjects + ListObjectsV2 hooked (via shared list_impl).
- 9762f7b — CreateBucket + HeadBucket + DeleteBucket hooked.
- 68e829d — CopyObject + multipart upload path (Create/Upload/Complete/Abort/ListParts/ListMultipartUploads).
- c18e6bf — ACL (Get/Put bucket+object) + Tagging (Get/Put/Delete bucket+object) + DeleteObjects.
- 7562a5d — PAB + CORS + BucketPolicy + Lifecycle + ObjectLock (config + retention + legal hold).

~45 customer S3 verbs now mint a cap-token on every SigV4-verified request when MANTAD_CAP_TOKEN_KEY_PATH is configured. The minted token carries the resolved AccessKey's tenant context (silo_id/tenant_shard/region via the slice 2 prereq) + the verb's permission bitmask + bucket-scope CapabilityResource. mantas3-s3 lib tests pass 79/79.

VERIFY SIDE (Storage trait) is filed as a new bead — that's the natural slice 3 deliverable: change the Storage trait to accept the minted token and verify before serving. Once that lands, the customer data plane is RFD 00014 §12-13 compliant end-to-end (mint at gateway, verify at storage tier in-process).

title: f365 slice 1: cap-token-v2 on mantas3 peer-replication RPC (internal port)
description:
First slice of monitor-reef-f365 (cap-token-v2 RFD 00014 §12-13 alignment).

Today: mantas3/cluster/src/internal.rs (897 lines, ~40 handlers under /_internal/*) has ZERO auth — peer-to-peer metadata + blob ops rely entirely on network isolation. Confirmed by re-reading the file: no Bearer check, no SigV4, no cap-token. The bead description for f365 names this gap explicitly.

Cap-token primitives are ready: manta-storage/crates/manta-common/src/cap_token.rs defines CapabilityClaim, CapabilityToken, CapabilitySigner, CapabilityVerifier, CapabilityKey, version 2, 4h max TTL, postcard wire. manta-storage-daemon already verifies them — reference impl exists.

SCOPE
1. Add CapabilityKey loading to mantas3 daemon config (file path; same key cluster-wide). Read at startup; persist sample with mode 0600 in dev mode if file is missing.
2. Build a CapabilitySigner + CapabilityVerifier from the loaded key and inject into InternalState.
3. axum middleware on /_internal/* (excluding /_internal/health + /metrics): require an Authorization header carrying a postcard+base64-encoded CapabilityToken. Verify against CapabilityExpectation { domain: Admin, resource: Cluster, permissions: all, tenant: cluster_service_tenant() }. Mismatch → 401.
4. Peer client (mantas3/cluster/src/peer.rs) mints a fresh token per call (sign_now with 60s TTL is plenty for an RPC round-trip; in practice a per-request mint is cheaper than caching+rotation logic).
5. Migration mode: env-var-gated permissive (verified-if-present, allowed-if-absent + WARN log) vs strict. Default strict on fresh deploys; operators flip permissive during rolling upgrade.

CLUSTER-SERVICE TENANT SENTINEL
TenantContext { silo_id: SiloId::from_uuid(Uuid::nil()), project_id: None, user_id: None, tenant_shard: 0, region: cluster_region }. Verifier matches the sentinel via the standard CapabilityExpectation::tenant equality; no special-casing in the cap_token API.

CAP-TOKEN CLAIM SHAPE (peer-replication)
- domain: CapabilityDomain::Admin
- resource: CapabilityResource::Cluster
- permissions: full bitmask (service identity)
- tenant: cluster-service sentinel (see above)
- TTL: 60 seconds (tight; reduces stolen-token replay window)
- nonce: random 64-bit per mint

VERIFICATION
On the receiver side, every /_internal/* request (excluding health/metrics) passes through a middleware that:
1. Pulls the Authorization header, expects 'Manta-Cap <base64>'.
2. base64-decodes, postcard-deserializes to CapabilityToken.
3. CapabilityVerifier::verify against the cluster-service expectation.
4. Mismatch / missing → 401 (Strict) or WARN + allow (Permissive).

NON-GOALS (file as f365 slice 2+ later)
- SigV4 → cap-token mint at customer-facing data plane.
- Storage trait verifying cap-tokens at the in-process boundary.
- Replacing the cluster-master bearer admin-token at /admin/v1/*.
- Rotation choreography (multi-key acceptance during key rotation).

VERIFICATION
- Unit tests for the middleware + verifier wiring against synthesized tokens.
- Integration test: spin up a 2-node cluster, peer A sends a replication call to peer B, assert (a) tokens travel on the wire, (b) tampering the token rejects with 401, (c) Permissive mode allows missing tokens with WARN.

DEPS
Depends on monitor-reef-f365 (parent umbrella).
close_reason: Shipped 2026-06-04 in manta-storage:surface-s3 commit bff646f. mantas3/cluster/src/cap_token_ext.rs (800+ lines) + InternalState/PeerClient/ClusterView/peer.rs wiring + mantad CLI args + telemetry registry accessor. 37/37 mantas3-cluster tests pass on build01 (RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=1.92, fdb feature). All round-1 + round-2 panel concerns folded: dedicated X-Manta-Capability header (not Authorization), 5-min TTL (not 60s — leaves margin against 60s clock-skew tolerance), cluster-service sentinel centralized to one constructor + one predicate, CapTokenPolicy bundles verifier/expectation/mode/metrics, 11 reason codes with structured 401 body + tracing event + labeled failures_total counter, Permissive-mode telemetry (mode_permissive gauge + permissive_allowed_total counter), env vars unified to MANTAD_* prefix. Backward-compat: cap_policy=None when MANTAD_CAP_TOKEN_KEY_PATH unset — matches pre-slice-1 behaviour for fresh deploys. PeerClient now mints via with_auth_opt(view.peer_auth()) at all 5 construction sites. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/f365-slice1-peer-cap-tokens.md.

title: zy0v slice 4: Prometheus alert rules (paging + non-paging)
description:
Fourth slice of monitor-reef-zy0v. Alert rules in code.

PAGING alerts (per parent bead):
- Availability < SLO over 5 min (per op).
- Any data-loss event (mantas3_prober_data_integrity_failures_total > 0).
- mantad process down on N nodes.
- Wedge detection: sum_over_time(mantas3_prober_cycle_success == 0)[3 cycles] from slice-1 prober.

NON-PAGING alerts (tickets):
- Slow burn on SLO budget.
- Disk-full forecast < 30d.
- Cert expiry < 30d.

Also: separate higher-severity rule for mantas3_prober_cycle_panics_total (prober itself broken) vs mantas3_prober_cycle_success == 0 (target broken). Per slice-1 R3.

DEPS
- Depends on zy0v-slice1 (prober metrics, including auth_failures and data_integrity counters)
- Depends on zy0v-slice2 (SLO targets determine thresholds)

PREREQ (open)
- Where alerts get routed (PagerDuty? Slack? operator email?) is unresolved.
close_reason: shipped manta-storage 03b47eb — ops/prometheus/alerts.yml, 6 groups / 20 rules. Each annotates runbook: pointing at docs/runbook.md section. Severity routing: page vs ticket. Covers availability fast/slow burn, durability (any integrity failure pages), wedge detection (69bg pattern + prober self-panic), latency p99 floors (small/large × get/put), saturation (rate-limit, FDB, heartbeat, disk, TLS), STS trust-policy rejection. Validates as YAML (python yaml.safe_load OK). Thresholds pin to docs/sli-slo.md.

title: zy0v slice 2: SLI/SLO definitions doc (manta-storage/docs/sli-slo.md)
description:
Second slice of monitor-reef-zy0v. Documentation-only — writes down the SLIs and SLOs the rest of zy0v measures against.

Scope (per parent bead):
- Availability: % of requests not 5xx, per operation, per minute, rolling-window. Target 99.9% per op per month.
- Latency: p99 PutObject + GetObject per payload bucket. Targets: PutObject p99 <50ms for <=64 KiB / <500ms for <=4 MiB; GetObject p99 <20ms for <=64 KiB / <200ms for <=4 MiB.
- Durability: zero data-loss events per quarter (paging on any).
- Saturation: queue depth / connection count / FDB latency relative to budget.

Targets above are placeholders pending real load-test data — slice 4 (alerts) and ge1c (3-node validation) feed back to refine them.

Lives in manta-storage/docs/sli-slo.md so it's adjacent to the system it measures. ~1-2 hours.

DEPS
- Depends on zy0v-slice1 (prober metrics names need to exist before they can be referenced in SLIs).

title: zy0v slice 1: mantas3 synthetic-request prober (Rust daemon, /metrics endpoint)
description:
First slice of monitor-reef-zy0v. Self-contained black-box prober that catches wedge-style failures like 69bg. Standalone Rust daemon at services/mantas3-prober/ talking to mantad:7443 via aws-sdk-s3. Runs PUT/GET/HEAD/DELETE every 30s, exposes Prometheus metrics on :9275.

Plan: ~/.claude/plans/zy0v-slice1-prober.md
Nine-reviewers: Approve round 2 (2026-06-04).

KEY DELIVERABLES
- services/mantas3-prober/Cargo.toml + src/{main,config,probe,metrics,supervise}.rs
- services/mantas3-prober/README.md (required vs tuning env vars, log schema, maintenance recipe)
- services/mantas3-prober/tests/wiremock_smoke.rs (tier 2)
- Tier 1 unit tests in src/probe.rs + src/metrics.rs
- ~250-400 lines total

INVARIANTS (from plan)
- I1: bucket-missing at startup -> fatal exit (SMF restart with backoff)
- I2: every SDK call wrapped in tokio::time::timeout(op_timeout, ...)
- I3: probe key is 'probe-<uuid4>', no timestamp
- I4: byte-exact body verification (data_integrity counter on mismatch)
- I5: stale-key cleanup is operator-owned (README documents sweep recipe)
- I6: auth failure (403) emits distinct auth_failures_total counter + WARN log

RELIABILITY (from plan)
- R1: cycle body in tokio::spawn; JoinError::is_panic caught, logged, cycle_panics_total++, loop continues
- R2: metrics listener supervised; if it dies, daemon exits non-zero (SMF restarts)
- R3: prober-vs-target separation in metrics (cycle_success vs cycle_panics_total)

METRICS SHAPE
- mantas3_prober_op_duration_seconds histogram with {op, outcome} labels (outcome: success|timeout|error_4xx|error_5xx|sdk_error)
- mantas3_prober_data_integrity_failures_total counter
- mantas3_prober_auth_failures_total counter
- mantas3_prober_cycle_panics_total counter
- mantas3_prober_cycle_success gauge
- mantas3_prober_cycle_interval_seconds gauge (alert rules can self-describe)

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (from round 2 panel, fold during build)
1. Verify metrics-exporter-prometheus exposes listener handle for R2 supervision. If not, swap to prometheus crate + ~30 lines of hyper.
2. Tier-1 tests for config_parse_failure_exits_non_zero and head_bucket_failure_exits_non_zero.
3. sdk_error_chain in log schema as structured array of {error_type, message}, not Rust Debug string.
4. PUT payload is fresh random bytes per cycle (no seeded RNG).
5. cycle_id is UUIDv4.
6. Emit mantas3_prober_cycle_interval_seconds gauge so PromQL alerts can self-describe.
7. Tier-1 test for the WARN-log-after-3-consecutive-403s property (I6).

VERIFICATION

- cargo test -p mantas3-prober --tests (tier 1 + 2)
- Tier 3 live smoke at 192.168.1.182:7443 (manual, post-deploy)

Release build clean. All I1-I6 invariants + R1-R3 reliability mechanisms implemented per the round-2 plan. Workspace deps added: aws-sdk-s3 v1 (rustls), aws-config v1, aws-credential-types v1, hyper v1, hyper-util, http-body-util, prometheus 0.13, tracing-subscriber 'json' feature.
