title: manta-storage S3 surface: DeleteObjects with empty Object list returns MalformedXML
description:
Found during full S3 compat sweep against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443 (2026-06-03).

manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-bulk-delete-test.sh exercises
DeleteObjects with an empty Object list and expects success (mantad
should treat 0 deletions as a no-op). Currently mantad returns:

  aws: An error occurred (MalformedXML) when calling the
       DeleteObjects operation: Unknown

92 of 93 sub-tests pass; only this empty-list case fails.

AWS S3 spec actually requires at least one Object element in the
DeleteObjects body — real AWS returns 'MalformedXML' too (the XSD
sets minOccurs=1 on <Object>). So mantad's current behavior arguably
matches AWS, and the test's expectation is the one that needs to
change. BUT:
  - Some popular S3 clients (mc, rclone) tolerate empty list as no-op
  - The behavioral parity with the legacy Node.js manta-buckets-api
    matters for migration testing

DECISION SHAPE:
  (a) Accept empty list as a no-op (returns empty Deleted[]) — matches
      old manta-buckets-api, helps client tolerance, diverges from
      AWS-strict
  (b) Keep MalformedXML (matches AWS) and update the test to expect it

PRIORITY: P3 — single edge case, both real S3 and our current
behavior reject it. Document the divergence and pick a side.

REPRO:
  aws s3api delete-objects --bucket <b> --delete '{"Objects":[]}'

OBSERVED:
  HTTP 400 MalformedXML 'Unknown'

title: manta-buckets-api test: s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh defaults to HTTPS but vnext endpoint is HTTP
description:
Found during full S3 compat sweep against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443 (2026-06-03).

The s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh script invokes s3cmd which defaults to
HTTPS. Mantad's S3 listener on the test rig is plain HTTP, so s3cmd
panics on the TLS handshake:

  ERROR: SSL certificate verification failure: [SSL: WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER]
         wrong version number (_ssl.c:1081)

Cascades into 7 of the 11 sub-tests failing (list, get, MPU x3, get ACL,
set ACL) because they share the misconfigured s3cmd client.

This is a TEST-SIDE issue in the manta-buckets-api repo; mantad does
nothing wrong here. The fix shape is one of:
  (a) generate s3cmd config with 'use_https = False' + 'host_base = ...'
      derived from the $S3_ENDPOINT scheme, OR
  (b) pass --no-check-certificate --no-ssl --host=... explicit flags

UPSTREAM REPO: manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh
NO mantad-side fix required.

VERIFICATION: the 4 sub-tests that don't go through s3cmd (s3cmd put,
s3cmd delete, s3cmd ls, s3cmd info) pass — proving the underlying
endpoint is fine.

PRIORITY: P3 — test-environment hygiene, not a product gap. File as
upstream PR against the manta-buckets-api test corpus.

title: mantas3-meta head_rate example uses reqwest without declaring dep
description: crates/mantas3/meta/examples/head_rate.rs:22 calls `reqwest::Client::builder()` but reqwest is not listed in [dependencies]/[dev-dependencies] of crates/mantas3/meta/Cargo.toml. `cargo test --no-run --workspace` fails with E0433. Fix options: (a) add `reqwest = { workspace = true }` (or similar) to dev-dependencies; (b) move/delete the example. Surfaced while building on build01 against surface-s3.
close_reason: Resolved in manta-storage commit 7c5c5fc on surface-s3 via workspace OpenSpec change CHG-200. fdb_smoke/fdb_rate examples separately tracked as workspace beads-5l6q.

title: phase-c-build.sh + phase-c-deploy.sh polish
description: (a) phase-c-build.sh: strip the mantad/tritond release binaries before scp (91MB -> ~18MB; saves minutes per deploy). (b) phase-c-deploy.sh: wire MANTAD_ROOT_ACCESS_KEY_ID + MANTAD_ROOT_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY env so SigV4 verification doesn't silently disable on fresh deploys. Captured in docs/vnext/s3-data-plane-workspace-gate.md as followups.
close_reason: phase-c-build.sh now strips bins after staging copy (91MB->~18MB tritond); phase-c-deploy.sh §4b generates+persists MANTAD_ROOT_ACCESS_KEY_ID+SECRET to /opt/mantad/etc/root-creds (mode 600) and exports them on mantad launch so SigV4 doesn't silently disable. Doc updated. sh -n clean.

title: Cache eviction + cluster-root fallback live verify
description: Followups documented in evidence/s3-presigner-tritond-verify-20260531.md: (a) cache eviction on drop_silo_tenant_storage — unit-tested but never exercised end-to-end. Drop+re-init the same tenant and verify the post-rebind presign AKID differs from the pre-drop AKID. (b) cluster-root presigner fallback — fleet-admin (Unscoped) mints a presign; URL AKID should equal cluster.presigner_access_key_id, not a per-workspace key.
close_reason: live-verified 2026-05-31. Cache eviction proven via pre/post AKID delta (MKIAQFXR vs MKIA5EKG); cluster-root fallback proven via Unscoped AKID == cluster.presigner_access_key_id. Surfaced bd n4w7 (cascade gap on drop_silo_tenant_storage). Evidence: evidence/s3-presigner-cache-fallback-verify-20260531.md. Verify: tools/s3-presigner-cache-fallback-verify.py.

title: Workspace-gate integration test harness (Phase 3 followup)
description: Build an aws-sdk-rust-driven integration test in manta-storage/crates/mantas3/s3/tests/workspace_gate.rs that spins up a MantadS3 service in-process (in-memory MetaStore + mock Storage + s3s SigV4 verifier), provisions 2 workspaces + 2 IAM users + access keys via direct meta-store puts, and exercises the cross-tenant probe matrix from the Phase 1 live verify. Removes the need to deploy to a dev cluster for every gate change. Deferred from Phase 3 (monitor-reef-64zl) because the in-process bootstrap is non-trivial; the live verify scripts cover the matrix end-to-end today.

title: Phase 3: workspace-gate migration tool + integration tests
description: (1) mantad-adm bucket repair-workspace --bucket NAME --workspace t-X stamps a workspace onto legacy bucket rows. (2) aws-sdk-rust integration test that spins up two synthetic IAM users with the same name in different workspaces and exercises the cross-tenant probes. See plan §Phase 3.

title: Consolidate workspace-archive paths in tenants.rs
description: delete_silo_tenant and drop_silo_tenant_storage both archive a bound mantad workspace before mutating the tenant row. Currently each handler hand-rolls the client_for + delete_workspace + audit-on-failure dance, with subtle differences in pre-flight (drop-storage pre-flights health, tenant-delete does not) and 404 handling (tenant-delete tolerates 404 as benign, drop-storage surfaces it). Extract the shared archive-on-mantad logic into one helper to reduce drift and align the two flows.

title: tritond: trim per-module use preludes in src/handlers/* (remove #![allow(unused_imports)], cargo fix --lib)
tritond: settings.rs env_str_key uses .expect() under clippy expect_used=deny -- cargo clippy -p tritond fails (pre-existing)
Design doc open question 4 references already-solved problem
description: Open question 4 asks about REPO_ROOT/TOP issue but ENGBLD_REPO_ROOT approach already addresses it. Either close the question or clarify what remains unsolved.

title: setup.sh: exit 0 instead of SMF_EXIT_OK on re-run
description: smf_include.sh sourced after the first-boot marker check guard. Early exit uses exit 0 not SMF_EXIT_OK. Move source before marker check for consistency.

title: image.defs.mk: git submodule update also silent on failure
description: shell git submodule update --init swallows exit code same as git rev-parse issue. ENGBLD_REQUIRE set to partial output on failure. Known Make shell limitation but worth guarding.

title: Hardcoded 127.0.0.1:8080 bind address
description: triton-api-server binds to localhost only - unreachable from other zones. SAPI template generates config.json but binary ignores it. Need to read bind address from config file when this becomes a real service.

title: Design doc open questions numbering skips 2
description: Open questions section goes 1, 3, 4, 5. Item 2 was apparently removed but remaining items not renumbered.

title: P3: safe_triton() doc comment says it checks stderr but code does not
description: In cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs, the safe_triton() doc comment (line 132-133) says 'asserts success and empty stderr' but the code does NOT assert empty stderr. The comment on line 150 explicitly says stderr is intentionally not checked. Fix: update the doc comment to match the actual behavior.

title: P3: delete_test_instance() discards deletion result with let _
description:
cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs line 304: let _ = run_triton_with_profile(["delete", "-f", "-w", &inst_id]) discards the deletion result. If deletion fails (instance in wrong state, API error), the failure is completely silent and the instance leaks. In CI with real cloud resources, leaked instances cost money.

Additionally, line 301: if the JSON parse of 'instance get' output fails, the function silently skips deletion entirely.

Fix: at minimum eprintln! if deletion fails. File: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs lines 291-306

title: P3: make_resource_name() has no randomness, risks collision in concurrent test runs
description:
cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs make_resource_name() generates names as {prefix}-{hostname} with no random or time-based suffix. If two test runs execute concurrently on the same machine, they generate identical resource names, causing creation conflicts or one test operating on another's resources. Fix: add std::process::id() or a short random suffix.
Line: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:96-101

title: P3: Truncated SHA1 hash in image_list.json fixture
description: cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/image_list.json has "sha1": "5dfeb0fba tried" which is not a valid SHA1 hash. SHA1 hashes should be 40-character hex strings. This appears to be a typo or garbled value from the original fixture creation.

title: Debug format on user-facing println: "Removed msg: {:?}" in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 131

Current code: println!("Removed msg: {:?}", msg.0)

Uses Debug format for user-facing println output. The inner type is BackendKey which has Display.

Fix: Change to println!("Removed msg: {}", msg.0)

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm output uses Display format.

title: Debug format on user-facing println: "Added msg: {:?}" in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 128

Current code: println!("Added msg: {:?}", msg.key)

Uses Debug format for user-facing println output. BackendKey derives both Debug and Display, so Display would give cleaner output (no quotes around the string).

Fix: Change to println!("Added msg: {}", msg.key)

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm output uses Display format.

title: Debug format anti-pattern: format!("{:?}", DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase() in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

Current code: format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()

This is the exact documented anti-pattern: using Debug format on an enum and then lowercasing. It relies on the Debug representation of slog::Level happening to produce the desired output.

Fix: slog::Level implements Display (which produces the lowercase string directly). Replace with:
  format!("{}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL) or util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL.to_string()

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm --help output still shows the correct default log level.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on RoleTagResource in role_tags.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs, line 25
Uses format\!("{:?}", resource_type) as fallback in user-facing error message.

Current code:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .map(|v| v.get_name().to_string())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| format\!("{:?}", resource_type));

Since RoleTagResource derives ValueEnum, to_possible_value() should never return None.
The {:?} fallback would produce "Instance" instead of "instance" if triggered.

Suggested fix - simplify since ValueEnum guarantees a value:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .expect("ValueEnum always has a value")
        .get_name()
        .to_string();

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli

title: 5 identical stale TODO comments in resolver functions
description:
Five identical '// TODO: should we add verification here that node-triton didn't have?' comments appear across resolver functions with no actionable detail:

- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/fwrule.rs:447
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:61
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs:568
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/vlan.rs:227
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/common.rs:15

These don't specify what verification is being considered (existence check? format validation?). A newcomer cannot act on them. Either convert to specific bead issues describing the exact behavior needed, or remove them.
close_reason: Replaced 5 stale TODOs with NOTE comments

title: TableBuilder::add_row() does not validate row length
close_reason: wontfix: add_row() is no longer used in production code — all table construction now goes through from_columns() and from_enum_columns(), which build rows internally with correct lengths. The only remaining add_row() call site is the test helper sample_builder(). Adding a debug_assert to protect a test-only path isn't worth the churn.

title: No tests for GetMachineError 410 Gone recovery logic
description: TypedClient::get_machine in clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs (lines 974-1013) implements special handling for HTTP 410 Gone responses where the server returns a Machine body instead of an Error body. The recovery logic catches Error::InvalidResponsePayload, tries to parse as Machine, and validates state == Deleted. There are no unit or integration tests for any GetMachineError variant (NotFound, JsonParse, UnexpectedRecovery). If the InvalidResponsePayload variant name changes in a Progenitor update, or if the MachineState::Deleted comparison breaks, the 410 recovery would silently fail.

title: Share/unshare image tests only cover empty ACL path
description:
In cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs, the payload tests for image share and unshare only test against a fake GET response with acl: None (empty ACL):

- test_image_share_payload: only validates adding a UUID to an empty ACL, never tests the deduplication path (sharing with an account already in the ACL)
- test_image_unshare_payload: only validates unsharing from an empty ACL (which is a no-op), never tests actual removal from a populated ACL

The share_image/unshare_image methods in cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs implement non-trivial ACL logic (dedup check, retain filter). If retain logic were inverted or dedup removed, no test would catch it.

title: TableBuilder sort silently ignores unknown field names
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/output/table.rs lines 105-109, the TableBuilder::print method's sort logic silently falls through when the user-provided sort field doesn't match any header name. A user typing 'triton ls -s nme' (typo for name) gets unsorted output with no feedback. Should emit a warning to stderr: eprintln\!("warning: unknown sort field '{}', output is unsorted", field). This is related to but distinct from monitor-reef-mjl1 which covers image.rs's custom sort logic.

title: emit-payload fixture loading silently swallows file/JSON errors
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs (debug-only code):

1. load_fixture() (lines 359-366): chains .ok()? on fs::read_to_string and serde_json::from_str. If TRITON_EMIT_PAYLOAD_FIXTURES env var points to a bad path or invalid JSON, silently falls through to hardcoded fakes with no warning.

2. fixture_response_body() (line 388): serde_json::to_string(template).ok()? silently swallows re-serialization errors.

3. emit_request_payload() (lines 287-291): non-JSON request body silently replaced with Value::Null, hiding malformed body content.

All are debug-only but cause confusing test failures. Should eprintln warnings when env var is set but file cannot be loaded/parsed.

title: GetMachineError::Client flattens all errors to String
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1011, the catch-all arm of get_machine converts Progenitor errors to String via e.to_string(), discarding the error variant and source chain:

    Err(e) => Err(GetMachineError::Client(e.to_string()))

This handles CommunicationError, InvalidRequest, PreHookError, etc. Downstream code cannot distinguish network failures from auth failures from server errors. Consider wrapping the original Error<types::Error> or at minimum preserving the error variant name in the string.

title: triton image create: different CLI syntax from node-triton
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-image-create

Rust syntax: triton image create <machine> --name <name> --version <version>
Node syntax: triton image create [--description=DESC] ... (no --name flag)

Node error: 'unknown option: "--name"'

The comparison test needs run_payload_test_split with different arguments for each CLI, or the Rust syntax should be investigated for compatibility.

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs

title: triton rbac role create: member/policy format differs from node-triton
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-rbac-role-create

Node sends members as full objects and policies as objects:
  "members": [{"default": false, "id": "testuser", "type": "subuser"}]
  "policies": [{"name": "testpolicy"}]

Rust sends them as simple strings:
  "members": ["testuser"]
  "policies": ["testpolicy"]

The API may accept both formats, but we should match node-triton's wire format for compatibility.

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_rbac.rs (or similar)

title: triton volume create: node-triton rejects size 10240 client-side
description:
Comparison test IDs: payload-volume-create, payload-volume-create-net

Node.js triton validates volume sizes client-side and rejects 10240 as invalid: 'size "10240" is not a valid volume size'. Rust triton passes the size through to the API.

This may indicate Rust should add client-side size validation, or the comparison test needs to use a size that node-triton accepts.

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_volumes.rs

title: triton network create: camelCase vs snake_case field names in payload
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-network-create-gw

When creating a fabric network with --gateway, Rust uses camelCase field names while Node.js uses snake_case:
- Node: provision_start_ip, provision_end_ip
- Rust: provisionStartIp, provisionEndIp

Node also sends empty 'resolvers': [] and 'routes': {} that Rust omits.

Diff:
-    "provision_end_ip": "10.0.0.254",
-    "provision_start_ip": "10.0.0.1",
-    "resolvers": [],
-    "routes": {},
+    "provisionEndIp": "10.0.0.254",
+    "provisionStartIp": "10.0.0.1",

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs (and potentially apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/ for the struct definition)
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs

title: triton network create: missing --gateway/--no-nat validation
description:
Comparison test ID: payload-network-create

Node.js triton requires either --gateway (-g) or --no-nat (-x) when creating a fabric network. Rust triton accepts the command without either flag and emits the payload.

Node error: 'without a --gateway (-g), you must specify --no-nat (-x)'
Rust: emits POST /fabrics/default/vlans/100/networks payload successfully

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs

title: resource_state_typed() doc claims it can return None but it cannot
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs:196-201, doc says returns None for unparseable states, but VmState::FromStr maps all unknowns to Ok(VmState::Unknown) so it always returns Some. Doc should be updated to reflect reality, noting Option is preserved for forward compat if FromStr ever becomes fallible.

title: update_image doc comment missing Share and Unshare actions
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/lib.rs:733-739, the update_image doc comment lists 4 actions but ImageAction enum has 6. The Share and Unshare actions are missing from the documentation.
close_reason: Added share/unshare note to update_image doc

title: ErrorResponse.code is untyped String requiring hardcoded string matching
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/common.rs:25-35, ErrorResponse.code is String. Consumer code (e.g. errors.rs ResourceNotFoundError) must match on string literals like ResourceNotFound, violating the No Hardcoded Enum Strings rule. Consider an ErrorCode enum with #[serde(other)] fallback, or at minimum typed constants.

title: Minor SSH silent fallbacks: port tag and HOME directory
description:
Two minor silent fallback issues in the SSH command:

1. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:130-132 — If the tritoncli.ssh.port tag contains a non-numeric value (e.g., 'abc'), .parse::<u16>().ok() silently discards the parse error and falls back to port 22. The user set a custom port tag and has no idea it's being ignored.

2. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:281 — std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| "/tmp".to_string()) falls back to /tmp when HOME is unset. The ControlPath will point to /tmp/.triton/tmp/ which may have unintended side effects and doesn't match the config_dir() resolution logic.

Fix: emit warnings for invalid port tags and use dirs::home_dir() consistently.

title: No end-to-end test for CertGenerator::generate()
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs (660 lines) has 10 unit tests for DER encoding helpers, but no test that exercises CertGenerator::generate() — the actual certificate generation flow. This module generates X.509 certificates for Docker TLS setup, a security-sensitive operation. The unit tests verify encoding primitives but do not verify that a generated certificate is actually valid or parseable by standard TLS libraries. Fix: add a test that generates a certificate with CertGenerator and verifies the result parses as valid X.509.

title: Missing serde tests for types with tricky rename attributes
description:
Several CloudAPI types with non-trivial serde configurations lack round-trip deserialization tests:

1. FirewallRule (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/firewall.rs) — has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")] hyphenated rename
2. SshKey (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs) — also has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")]
3. Package and DiskSize (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs) — DiskSize is #[serde(untagged)] which is fragile with serde (variant ordering matters)
4. Changefeed types (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs) — multiple rename strategies (rename_all = "lowercase", rename_all = "snake_case") and the canonical #[serde(other)] pattern

The commit history shows serde rename bugs are real (multiple commits fix snake_case vs camelCase issues). Add fixture-based serde round-trip tests for these types.
close_reason: Added serde tests for FirewallRule (role-tag), SshKey (role-tag), Package (role-tag + flexible_disk), and DiskSize (untagged enum).

title: triton volume JSON output missing empty tags field
description:
Comparison test IDs: volume-list-json, volume-get-json
Node.js includes 'tags: {}' in volume JSON output, Rust omits it.

The Volume struct uses #[serde(default)] on the tags field, which means empty maps deserialize correctly but are not serialized back to the output. Need to ensure empty tags are included in JSON output to match Node.js behavior.

Likely source file: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs (tags field serialization)
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_volumes.rs

Diff (volume-list-json and volume-get-json identical):
   "state": "ready",
-  "tags": {},
   "type": "tritonnfs"

title: triton volume list size formatting differs from Node.js
description:
Comparison test ID: volume-list
What Node.js produces: 10G
What Rust produces: 10240 MiB

Node.js formats volume sizes as human-readable gibibyte strings (e.g., '10G') in table output, while Rust shows raw MiB values (e.g., '10240 MiB').

Likely source file: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs
Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_volumes.rs

Diff:
-SHORTID   NAME          SIZE  TYPE       STATE  AGE
-e3b60940  compare-test  10G   tritonnfs  ready  2m
+SHORTID   NAME          SIZE       TYPE       STATE  AGE
+e3b60940  compare-test  10240 MiB  tritonnfs  ready  2m

title: triton image list -j: missing image_size, min_memory, min_ram
description:
Comparison test ID: image-list-json
- Rust is missing image_size field on zvol/bhyve images
- Rust is missing min_memory and min_ram from requirements on some images
- Likely source: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs (Image struct, ImageRequirements)
- Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs

Diff (image-list-json):
-    "image_size": 10240,
     "name": "almalinux-8",

-      "min_memory": 500,
-      "min_ram": 500

title: triton get commands: match Node.js JSON indent and key ordering
description:
Comparison test IDs: image-get, package-get, network-get
- Rust uses 2-space indent, Node uses 4-space indent for JSON get output
- Rust sorts keys alphabetically, Node preserves insertion order
- The -json variants (with jq normalization) all PASS, confirming data is correct
- Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs or per-command formatting
- Need to match Node's 4-space JSON formatting for non -j get output

Diff example (package-get):
-    "default": false,
+  "default": false,
close_reason: Fixed 4-space indent in print_json_pretty. Key ordering is a separate issue filed as a new bead.

title: triton instance: missing block_size in disks, extra flags field
description:
Comparison test IDs: instance-list-json, instance-get-json, instance-get
- Rust is missing block_size field in disk objects (Node has block_size: 8192)
- Rust has extra flags: "-" field on one instance that Node doesn't include
- Likely source: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs (Disk struct), cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance.rs
- Rust test to add in: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_instances.rs

Diff (instance-get-json):
-      "block_size": 8192,
       "boot": true,
+    "flags": "-",

title: Exit code 3 for not-found errors not implemented
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton uses exit code 3 for "resource not found" errors (documented
in help text). Rust only uses 0, 1, 2 (where 2 is clap usage errors).

This may affect scripts that check exit codes to distinguish "error" from
"not found".

## Fix

When an API call returns 404 or a resource lookup fails, exit with code 3
instead of 1. This requires catching the specific error type before it
reaches the generic anyhow error handler in main.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` — error handling / exit code logic
- May need a custom error type or match on HTTP status codes

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_error_paths.rs`
- Assert `triton instance get nonexistent-uuid` exits with code 3
- Assert other errors still exit with code 1

Differences.md issue #16.

title: triton env missing --triton/--docker/--smartdc/--unset flags
description:
## Summary

Node.js `triton env` supports flags to control which sections are emitted:
- `-t/--triton` — emit only triton section
- `-d/--docker` — emit only docker section
- `-s/--smartdc` — emit only smartdc section
- `-u/--unset` — emit unset commands instead of export

Rust only has `-s/--shell` to choose shell type (bash/fish). All sections
are always emitted and there is no unset mode.

## Fix

Add the missing flags to the env command. The `-s` flag conflicts — Node
uses `-s` for `--smartdc` while Rust uses `-s` for `--shell`. Need to
decide on flag mapping. Suggest using `--shell` (long only) for shell
type to free up `-s` for `--smartdc` compatibility.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs`

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs` (create if needed)
- Assert `--triton` emits only the triton section
- Assert `--unset` emits `unset` commands

Differences.md issue #11.

title: Top-level aliases differ from Node.js (ls, instances, rm)
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton has top-level shortcut commands that Rust implements differently:

| Alias       | Node.js                    | Rust                        |
|-------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------|
| ls          | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| instances   | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| rm          | Shortcut for instance delete | Alias on delete (works)   |

The Rust versions work but are implemented as aliases on different commands,
which may cause minor behavioral differences (help text, error messages).

Additionally, Rust adds `svcs` as an alias for `services` which Node.js
doesn't have (Node returns "unknown command" for svcs). This is additive
and not a bug.

## Fix

Verify that `ls`, `instances`, and `rm` produce identical output to their
Node.js equivalents. If there are differences in behavior (not just help
text), fix them. If behavior matches, this can be closed as compatible.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` or wherever top-level commands are defined

## Test
- Comparison framework alias tests (API tier) already cover `insts`, `imgs`,
  `pkgs`, `nets`. Could add `ls` and `rm` alias tests.

Differences.md issue #13.
close_reason: compatible: ls, instances, and rm aliases all route to the exact same handler functions (instance::list::run and instance::delete::run) with identical argument structs. The only difference is help text, which is expected for top-level shortcuts vs nested subcommands. 225 subcommand alias tests pass confirming all aliases work correctly.

title: profile set-current output message differs from Node.js
description:
## Summary

Node.js outputs contextual messages:
- `Set "X" as current profile` (when changing)
- `"X" is already the current profile` (when already set)

Rust always prints: `Current profile: X` — no distinction between
setting a new profile vs confirming the current one.

## Fix

Check if the requested profile is already current before setting.
Print the appropriate message in each case.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs` — around line 503

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs`
- Assert message is `Set "X" as current profile` when changing
- Assert message is `"X" is already the current profile` when already set

Differences.md issue #5.

title: triton image copy missing 'cp' alias
description:
Comparison test ID: help-image

Node.js 'triton image' has 'copy (cp)' but Rust only has 'copy' without the 'cp' alias.
'triton image cp' fails with 'unrecognized subcommand'.

Fix: add visible_alias = "cp" to the copy subcommand in the image command.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs (copy subcommand definition)
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs

title: triton subcommand help text differs from Node.js (all 9 subcommands)
description:
Comparison test IDs: help-instance, help-image, help-package, help-network, help-volume, help-key, help-fwrule, help-vlan, help-account

All subcommand --help outputs differ between Node.js and Rust due to clap vs node-cmdln layout:
- Node: longer descriptions, Usage with 'COMMAND [ARGS...]', Options before Commands
- Rust: shorter descriptions, '<COMMAND>' syntax, Commands before Options, aliases in [aliases: ...]
- Rust shows -j/--json at subcommand group level; Node does not
- Different command ordering and description wording

Same class of difference as top-level help (already in ignored-diffs.txt). These may also be intentional clap-layout divergences.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/*.rs (clap derive attributes)
Rust test files: various cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_*.rs
close_reason: Help tests now use command-coverage normalization; all 9 subcommands pass

title: triton completion bash generates clap_complete output instead of node-dashdash
description:
Comparison test ID: completion-bash

Completely different bash completion scripts: Node.js uses node-dashdash (2,281 lines), Rust uses clap_complete (13,240 lines). These are fundamentally different completion systems.

This is likely an intentional divergence since clap_complete is the standard for clap-based CLIs. May want to move to ignored-diffs.txt.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/completion.rs
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_completion.rs (create if needed)
close_reason: Intentional: clap_complete vs node-dashdash is by design

title: triton env --shell fish produces output where Node.js fails
description:
Comparison test ID: env-fish

Node.js exits 1 (does not support fish shell), while Rust exits 0 and produces valid fish shell output. This is technically an improvement in the Rust CLI.

Node.js: exit code 1, no output
Rust:    exit code 0, produces valid fish set -gx commands

This may be intentional. Consider whether to mark as ignored-diff or keep tracking.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs (create if needed)
close_reason: Intentional: Rust adds fish shell support that Node.js lacks

title: triton env uses single quotes instead of double quotes (bash)
description:
Comparison test ID: env-bash

Rust CLI uses single quotes in bash export statements, Node.js uses double quotes.

Node.js: export TRITON_PROFILE="env"
Rust:    export TRITON_PROFILE='env'

Both are valid bash, but for compatibility the Rust output should match Node.js double-quote style.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs (create if needed)

title: triton profile list -j shows insecure instead of curr field
description:
Comparison test ID: profile-list-json

Rust JSON output includes 'insecure' field instead of 'curr' field in profile list.

Node.js: {"account": "nshalman", "curr": true, ...}
Rust:    {"account": "nshalman", "insecure": false, ...}

The 'curr' (current profile) boolean is missing from Rust output and replaced by 'insecure'. Both fields may be needed, or 'curr' may need to be added to the JSON serialization.

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs (JSON output formatting)
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs

title: triton profile get exits 1 with no output (differs from Node.js)
description:
Comparison test IDs: profile-get, profile-get-json

The Rust triton CLI exits with code 1 and produces no output for 'triton profile get env' and 'triton profile get -j env', while Node.js exits 0 and shows profile details.

Node.js output (profile-get):
  name: env
  account: nshalman
  curr: true
  keyId: SHA256:...
  url: https://us-central-1.api.mnx.io/

Rust output: (empty, exit code 1)

Likely source: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs
Rust test file: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs

title: Profile create/delete workflow tests
description: Only read-only profile tests exist (profile get env, profile list). No tests for profile create or profile delete workflows. Requires careful temp dir management to avoid affecting real profile configs.

title: Changefeed WebSocket command tests
description: The changefeed command is WebSocket-based and has zero test coverage. Testing would require a mock WebSocket server or a dedicated test fixture. Consider adding at minimum help/existence tests and optionally a mock-server integration test.

title: Instance audit command tests
description: The instance audit command has no tests - not even help/existence tests in cli_subcommands.rs. Add help tests and API integration tests verifying the audit trail endpoint returns expected format.

title: RBAC API integration tests
description: No functional tests exist for RBAC operations against a real CloudAPI. When access to an RBAC-enabled CloudAPI is available, add integration tests for user/role/policy CRUD operations, rbac apply workflow, and role-tag management. These should follow the existing ignored-test pattern gated by config.json.

title: Volume create default size calculation may use wrong units
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs:278-283 - Comment says Convert GB to MB but multiplies by 1024. Unclear whether API s.size is in GB or GiB or already MiB. If already MiB, multiplication produces volume 1024x too large. Needs verification against actual API.

title: 410 Gone recovery catches all InvalidResponsePayload errors
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:525-529 - The get_machine error handler catches InvalidResponsePayload which does not carry HTTP status code. It fires for any response failing to parse, not just 410. Low practical risk but comment is misleading.

title: No Machine round-trip serde test and no action-dispatch type tests
description: apis/cloudapi-api/tests/serde_machine.rs has no round-trip test for Machine type. Also no serde tests for action-dispatch types (MachineAction, ImageAction, DiskAction, VolumeAction) despite being a key documented pattern. Existing round-trip tests only check field subsets.

title: run_triton() test helper discards exit status
description: cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:32-46 - run_triton() returns (stdout, stderr) but discards exit status. A crash could produce empty stdout that still passes pattern-match assertions. run_triton_with_env() correctly returns status as third element.
close_reason: Fixed: run_triton() now returns (stdout, stderr, success) matching run_triton_with_env()

title: No offline error-path testing for CLI commands
description: All offline (non-ignored) CLI tests only verify help text. Missing: required argument validation, invalid argument combinations, malformed input handling, exit code verification for error cases. These can run without API access.
close_reason: Added 41 offline error-path tests in cli_error_paths.rs covering missing-arg, invalid-arg, and variadic-arg behavior

title: AGENTS.md documentation gaps
description:
From review 1A:
- Architecture section omits quickcheck-helpers, rebalancer-legacy, sharkspotter from libs/
- Common Make Targets table omits service-build/test, client-build/test
- Type safety audit skill does not cover Rules 6 (Re-export Pattern) and 8 (Field Naming Exceptions)
- docs/design/checked-in-client-generation.md not referenced from AGENTS.md or tutorials

title: format_age produces misleading 0s for future timestamps
description: cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs:84-126 - Clock skew between servers can cause negative durations that display as 0s instead of a clear indicator like - or future.

title: Volume parse_tags silently ignores malformed tags
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs:237-257 - Tags without = are silently skipped. The equivalent code in image.rs returns an error for malformed tags. Volume command should be consistent.

title: Inconsistent enum_to_display() vs Display usage in migration.rs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/migration.rs - Uses {} (Display) in 10 places and enum_to_display() in 6 places within the same file. Both produce correct output but AGENTS.md says to use enum_to_display() for serde enums.

title: Password metadata fields (_pw) silently dropped in create_machine
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1050-1060 - Metadata keys ending in _pw are removed during legacy transformation with no warning and no code path to transmit them. User-provided data silently lost.

title: Fragile is_check arg detection in openapi-manager
description: openapi-manager/src/main.rs:118 - std::env::args().any(|a| a == "check") scans ALL args including binary path. Should restrict to subcommand position with args().nth(1).as_deref() == Some("check").

title: certgen not_after calculation can panic on overflow
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs:174 - SystemTime + Duration panics on overflow. While lifetime_days is u32 and practically limited, calling generate() with extreme values would panic. Should use checked_add.

title: Workspace dependency inconsistencies in triton-cli and triton-auth
description: cli/triton-cli/Cargo.toml: thiserror, tracing-subscriber, uuid, chrono, tempfile use direct version strings instead of workspace = true. The uuid pin to 1.16 vs workspace 1 is most notable. libs/triton-auth/Cargo.toml: thiserror = 2.0 should be workspace = true.

title: resolve_instance does not check for ambiguous short-IDs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:67-80 - Returns first prefix match without checking for duplicates. If two machines share the same 8-char UUID prefix, the wrong machine could be silently operated on. Should collect all matches and error if ambiguous.

title: resolve_instance short-ID lookup does not verify hex input
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:67-80 - Any name >= 8 chars triggers unnecessary list-all-machines API call before falling through to name lookup. resolve_package in create.rs already correctly checks chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()). Performance issue for instance names >= 8 chars.

title: Private key files written with default permissions
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs:586-587 and :728-729 - Docker/CMON private keys written with fs::write which uses umask-dependent permissions. Should explicitly set 0o600 on Unix for private key material.

title: create_machine bypasses Progenitor pre-hook (auth duplication)
description: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:360-439 - create_machine builds its own HTTP request, duplicating auth logic from auth.rs. The RBAC URL-encoding bug (monitor-reef-rij) is a direct consequence. Future auth changes require dual maintenance. Long-term: refactor to use Progenitor hooks.
close_reason: Fixed in 563e52a - create_machine now uses Progenitor pre-hook instead of manual HTTP

title: VmAction enum missing #[serde(other)] Unknown
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/vms.rs:89-108 - Inconsistent with all other deserializable enums in the crate (VmState, Brand, JobExecution, MigrationState, MigrationPhase all have Unknown). Lower risk since it is primarily an outbound request parameter.

title: Snapshot.state is Option<String> instead of typed enum
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/vms.rs:418-428 - Every other state field in vmapi-api uses a typed enum with #[serde(other)] Unknown, but Snapshot.state uses Option<String>. Should define a SnapshotState enum.

title: Inconsistent rename_all between Create/Update FabricNetworkRequest
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs:155 vs 184 - CreateFabricNetworkRequest uses snake_case, UpdateFabricNetworkRequest uses camelCase. Same resource should use consistent wire format. Needs verification against Node.js CloudAPI.

title: cueball-postgres-connection still uses native-tls directly
description: libs/cueball-postgres-connection/Cargo.toml depends on native-tls and postgres-native-tls directly. This is the last remaining native-tls usage in the workspace (after tokio-tungstenite was switched to rustls in c008acd). Investigate switching to rustls for the postgres connection as well to fully eliminate native-tls from the dependency tree.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: May be resolved by the modernization-skill branch, which replaces cueball with a different crate entirely. Check when that branch lands.

title: Restify conversion skill not mentioned in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not mention the restify-conversion skill, which is available for migrating Node.js Restify services to Rust. This skill is useful for contributors doing migration work. Fix: add a reference to the restify-conversion skill in the relevant AGENTS.md section.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: WebSocket/channel endpoint guidance absent from AGENTS.md

comments.author: Nahum Shalman

title: Beads/type-safety-audit cross-reference missing in AGENTS.md

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Field naming exceptions undocumented in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md does not document the conventions around field naming exceptions (e.g., when serde rename attributes override the default naming). Contributors may not know when/how to use rename attributes. Fix: add a section documenting field naming conventions and exception patterns.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Stale repository structure diagram in AGENTS.md
description: AGENTS.md contains a repository structure diagram that does not reflect the current directory layout. Missing or renamed directories are shown. Fix: update the diagram to match the actual repo structure.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1A: documentation consistency review. Rated LOW.

title: Mixed TLS backends (reqwest=rustls, tokio-tungstenite=native-tls)
description: Workspace uses rustls for reqwest but native-tls for tokio-tungstenite. This means two TLS implementations are compiled and linked, increasing binary size and attack surface. Fix: unify on a single TLS backend (preferably rustls) across all crates.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 1B: build config & CI review. Rated LOW — nice-to-have unification.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on slog::Level in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

This is the canonical anti-pattern: `format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()` — formatting an enum with Debug and calling .to_lowercase().

Code:
```rust
format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()
```

slog::Level is an external library type so enum_to_display() is not directly applicable. However, the pattern is fragile — Debug output format is not guaranteed stable.

Suggested fix: Use slog::Level display string directly if available, or define a const for the default level name string.

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver`.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on MigrationState/Phase in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1555, 1568, 1569

MigrationState and MigrationPhase enums are displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of wire-format strings.

Examples:
- Line 1555: `println!("{}: {:?} ({:?})", m.vm_uuid, m.state, m.phase);`
- Line 1568: `println!("  State: {:?}", migration.state);`
- Line 1569: `println!("  Phase: {:?}", migration.phase);`

Debug format produces "Running"/"Begin" while wire format is "running"/"begin" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", m.vm_uuid, enum_to_display(&m.state), enum_to_display(&m.phase));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify migration output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on JobExecution in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1153, 1166, 1184, 1205

JobExecution enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} in four places instead of the wire-format string.

Examples:
- Line 1153: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`
- Line 1166: `println!("Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1184: `println!("  Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1205: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`

Debug format produces "Succeeded" while wire format is "succeeded" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {}", job.uuid, enum_to_display(&job.execution));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify job execution output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on Brand in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, line 807

Brand enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string.

Code: `println!("Brand: {:?}", vm.brand);`

Debug format produces "Bhyve" while wire format is "bhyve" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("Brand: {}", enum_to_display(&vm.brand));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify brand output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on VmState in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 779, 808, 1525

VmState enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string. VmState has a Display impl in apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs, but the CLI should use enum_to_display() for consistency with triton-cli.

Examples:
- Line 779: `println!("{}: {} ({:?})", vm.uuid, alias_str, vm.state);`
- Line 808: `println!("State: {:?}", vm.state);`
- Line 1525: `println!("  {}: {:?}", uuid, status.state);`

Debug format produces "Running" while wire format is "running". This is inconsistent with triton-cli output.

Suggested fix: Use enum_to_display() from triton-cli output module, or add a similar helper to vmapi-cli:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", vm.uuid, alias_str, enum_to_display(&vm.state));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Hardcoded "unknown" fallback strings in CLI output code
description:
Files:
- cli/cloudapi-cli/src/main.rs:288,309 — `.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string())`
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:946,987 — same pattern
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs:143 — `.unwrap_or("unknown")`
- cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:94 — same pattern

**Problem:** Multiple CLI files use hardcoded `"unknown"` as fallback for optional enum values. Inconsistent with the `Unknown` variants that already exist on state enums.

**Suggested fix:** Define `const UNKNOWN_DISPLAY: &str = "unknown"` in a shared output module (e.g., `cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs`), or better yet, leverage the `Unknown` enum variants directly by making the state fields non-optional where possible.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-cli`
close_reason: Added opt_enum_to_display helper and UNKNOWN_DISPLAY constant

title: Action dispatch enums missing Clone+Serialize derives
description:
Files:
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs — MachineAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs — ImageAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs — VolumeAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — DiskAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/vmapi-api (VmAction): `Debug, Clone, Deserialize, JsonSchema` — missing Serialize

**Problem:** Action dispatch enums lack `Clone` and `Serialize` derives. While these are only used in request body deserialization (not CLI-facing), missing derives reduce testability and round-trip capability.

**Suggested fix:** Add `Clone, Serialize` to all action dispatch enums for consistency with other enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api`
close_reason: Added Clone+Serialize derives to all action dispatch enums

title: HTTP 410 Gone handling bypasses Progenitor client with 70 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 551-620

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns HTTP 410 Gone with a Machine body (not an error) for deleted machines. Progenitor treats 410 as an error and fails to parse. Workaround: 70 lines of manual HTTP handling that bypasses Progenitor entirely, including:
- Manual `get_machine()` method with direct reqwest usage
- Custom `GetMachineError` enum with `NotFound` variant
- Bypasses Progenitor response parsing

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor response override capabilities. May require upstream feature request for non-error 4xx body handling.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — deleted machine queries should still work.
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Progenitor call, catch InvalidResponsePayload for 410 recovery

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Fixed by wrapping Progenitor get_machine() and catching InvalidResponsePayload (which surfaces when 410 Gone body can't be deserialized as Error). Reduced from 70 lines of manual HTTP/auth code to a 24-line match on the Progenitor error. Also removed cloudapi-cli (dead code, superseded by triton-cli) and switched all CLI callers to use the typed wrapper.

title: Hardcoded enum string "failed" in rbac/apply.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs, line 797

**Current code:**
- `status: "failed".to_string()` in `ApplyChangeResult`

**Problem:** The `status` field in `ApplyChangeResult` is a `String` that takes values "ok", "failed", "skipped". These should be a typed enum.

**Suggested fix:** Define an `ApplyStatus` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum ApplyStatus {
    Ok,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
}
```
Then change `ApplyChangeResult.status` from `String` to `ApplyStatus`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded status strings with ApplyStatus enum

title: buckets-api integration scripts predate AWS S3 surface migration (legacy Manta URLs)
description:
Investigation umbrella from 2026-06-08.

manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-*.sh use legacy Manta URL path shape:
  ${S3_ENDPOINT}/${MANTA_USER}/buckets/$BUCKET/objects/$KEY

mantad (the AWS S3 surface) parses URLs path-style:
  ${ENDPOINT}/$BUCKET/$KEY

When the scripts hit mantad with legacy URLs, the first segment (the account name) is interpreted as the bucket → 404 NoSuchBucket on every direct-curl path. SDK paths via aws_s3api use the correct shape and work fine; only the raw curl paths fail.

Closed sibling beads (all 2026-06-08 buckets-api script triage):
- monitor-reef-2a9f — OPTIONS preflight 'no CORS headers' (legacy URL + tested before PUT CORS)
- monitor-reef-mg2q — DeleteObjects empty list (script error-keyword too narrow; mantad correctly returns MalformedXML per AWS spec)
- monitor-reef-pwn0 — anonymous public-ACL 404 (legacy URL)

If the buckets-api test suite needs to pass against mantad, the scripts need a sweep to migrate URL construction. Affected files (rg -l 'MANTA_USER}/buckets/' test-scripts/):
- s3-cors-test.sh (lines 71)
- s3-acl-access-test.sh (lines 237, 299)
- likely others — full grep above

Not blocking ship: aws_s3api / boto3 / mc / rclone / s3_suite.py all use AWS S3 path-style and pass. This umbrella tracks the script-side migration only.

P4 — operator/QA workflow improvement; not a customer-facing gap.

title: mantad: DeleteObjects (bulk delete) edge case on empty Objects list
description:
manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-bulk-delete.sh has one failing case on rig 2026-06-08: POST /$BUCKET/?delete with an empty <Delete><Quiet>false</Quiet></Delete> body (no <Object> elements) returns an error where AWS S3 / s3s spec says it should return an empty result successfully.

AWS behavior: DeleteObjects with zero Objects → 200 with empty <DeleteResult/>.
mantad behavior: 400 MalformedXML or similar (need to capture exact error from re-run).

Likely source: crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/delete_objects.rs — input validation rejects empty list rather than treating it as a no-op.

P4 — low-impact (no real client sends empty bulk-delete) but it's a spec deviation and the script triggers it.
close_reason:
wontfix: mantad behavior matches AWS S3 spec.

Tracing s3s 0.13.0:
- src/xml/generated_minio.rs:3205-3228 — Delete::deserialize_content requires objects.ok_or(DeError::MissingField); empty <Delete> body returns MissingField.
- src/http/de.rs:217-218 — DeError::MissingField wrapped as S3ErrorCode::MalformedXML.
- delete_bulk.rs handler never sees the empty list; rejection happens at parse time.

AWS S3 DeleteObjects documents Delete.Objects as 'Required' (≥1 Object element). boto3/AWS SDK enforces the same — empty list is rejected client-side or returns MalformedXML server-side.

The buckets-api script test_bulk_delete_empty_request (line 312-345) matches errors against 'empty|no.*object|invalid' but mantad returns 'MalformedXML' which doesn't match any of those keywords. The script's error matcher is too narrow — it should match the HTTP 400 + S3 error code 'MalformedXML' explicitly.

No mantad change needed. If the script needs to pass, the fix is in the script's matcher, not mantad.

title: .gitignore *.tar.gz pattern is repo-wide
description: Comment says eng buildimage targets but *.tar.gz matches any tarball anywhere in repo. Consider scoping to images/**/*.tar.gz or updating comment to note broad scope.

title: Design doc TODO references local filesystem path
description: TODO in zone-image-builds.md references ~/Workspace/jenkins-joylib/... which is one developers local path. Remove or replace with PR/commit reference once pushed.

title: ExportImageRequest has redundant rename_all = camelCase
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:263, ExportImageRequest has #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] at the struct level, but its only field (manta_path) has an explicit #[serde(rename = "manta_path")] override. The struct-level attribute is harmless but misleading -- a reader might think the wire format is camelCase when it's actually snake_case. Consider removing the struct-level rename_all since every field already has an explicit rename.

title: Stale and misleading comments in cloudapi-client lib.rs
description:
Several comments in clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs are inaccurate or stale:

1. Line 1596: Section header says 'Infallible serialization helper' but to_json_value panics on failure. In Rust, 'infallible' means cannot fail. Should say 'Serialization helper'.

2. Lines 298-308: emit_and_fake_get_response doc says 'returns {id: lastSeg, name: lastSeg} for single-resource GETs' but the function now dispatches to typed fake response builders via fake_response_body. The {id, name} pattern is only the final fallback for unknown operations.

3. Lines 781-787: TypedClient doc says it provides methods for 'machines, images, volumes, disks' but omits RBAC operations, create_machine, list_machines_with_tags, and get_machine (with 410 handling).
close_reason: Fixed 3 stale comments in cloudapi-client lib.rs

title: JSON key ordering: Rust alphabetical vs Node.js insertion order
description: Comparison test IDs: image-get, network-get, instance-get. After fixing 4-space indent (bg6), these tests still DIFF due to key ordering. Rust Progenitor-generated structs serialize fields alphabetically, while Node.js preserves API response order. Fixing requires either serde_json preserve_order feature + raw HTTP response bodies, or normalizing key order in the comparison script.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: instance-get also has the same JSON key ordering diff — added to known-diffs.txt
